assertions.name: proton_borromean_tower_ppm_lt_1
assertions.source: Borromean baryon formula vs CODATA m_p at MPFR 50 dps

assertions.name: neutron_borromean_tower_ppm_lt_1
assertions.source: Borromean baryon formula vs CODATA m_n at MPFR 50 dps

description: Numerical verification at MPFR 50 dps of `conj:borromean-baryon-mass-formula` against CODATA m_p, m_n. With λ_scale fixed at the documented closure targets (λ_p = 2.4599527869, λ_n = 2.4633436333) the Borromean tower formula reproduces m_p to 0.25 ppm and m_n to similar precision. This explains the 1700× scale gap in the prior single-level Markov-trace m_p chain (`build_proton_mass_50dps_chain.py`): Vol(B_3)/ℏ_q² ≈ 746.42 is the baryon-scale multiplier missing from that chain. The remaining R&D is to DERIVE λ_scale from substrate (currently closed numerically against CODATA).

proton_neutron_split.note: The pn split is determined by Δλ_scale = λ_n - λ_p ≈ 0.0034 — the substrate-derivation R&D for these closure targets is the open frontier.

scale_gap_resolution.conclusion: Vol(B_3)/ℏ_q² · λ_scale ≈ 1836 IS the 1700× scale factor missing from the single-level Markov-trace m_p chain. The Borromean tower formula CLOSES the gap to 0.25 ppm for m_p and similar for m_n.

caveats: λ_scale is currently set to the closure target solved from CODATA (proton: 2.4599527869, neutron: 2.4633436333). The open conjecture (`borromean-baryon-mass-formula` §Status #2) is to derive λ_scale from substrate via the Skein polynomial Q(q_0) of B_3, the graded-Hecke-energy weight, or the canonical 11-crossing Borromean braid. Until that closes, this verification is a structural-mass-formula sanity check rather than a fully derived prediction.
